medications.drug_name: intravenous adenosine (in-hospital) - if vagal manoeuvres fail
medications.indication_rationale: If a vagal manoeuvre fails, adenosine is the first-line pharmacological termination of stable SVT, but it must be given in a monitored setting with continuous ECG - not prescribed or given over telehealth.
contraindications_in_this_case: Administer only in a monitored in-person setting; not a telehealth medication

medications.indication_rationale: Caffeine (double espressos) is a precipitant that lowers the arrhythmia threshold and should be eliminated; this is a trigger to avoid rather than a therapy.
contraindications_in_this_case: Cease high-dose caffeine/energy drinks to reduce episode frequency

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Waiting it out at home indefinitely / self-driving to hospital
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: A sustained SVT needs rhythm capture and termination; waiting risks decompensation and self-driving while near-fainting risks collapse at the wheel.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Attempt a coached modified Valsalva manoeuvre now (bear down against a closed airway for ~15 seconds while lying, then lie flat and raise the legs); if it does not work, arrange urgent assessment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: A modified Valsalva safely terminates many episodes of stable SVT and is an appropriate first-line telehealth-coachable step.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop high-dose caffeine and energy drinks (e.g. double espressos) from now on.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Eliminating caffeine reduces the frequency of future SVT episodes.

patient_education_points.point: You have an episode of a fast, regular heart rhythm (SVT) - an 'electrical short-circuit'. Let's first try a simple breathing/bearing-down manoeuvre that can reset it. If that doesn't work, you'll need an urgent heart tracing and possibly a quick medication in hospital to reset the rhythm.

Please don't drive yourself while your heart is racing and you feel like you might faint on standing - arrange a friend or assisted transport. Call 000 if you get severe or crushing chest pain, become very breathless, or feel you're about to faint.

Once this settles, stopping high-dose caffeine (double espressos) will help reduce future episodes, and you should see a cardiologist about Holter monitoring and whether a small procedure (ablation) could cure it. (Same advice applies whether delivered by SMS or in the consult.)

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person assessment for ECG/termination; routine GP review within 72 hours for a baseline ECG; cardiology referral for Holter monitoring and electrophysiology study/ablation
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Rhythm capture and successful termination, ECG pattern (narrow-complex regular tachycardia), Holter/electrophysiology assessment for curative ablation, trigger avoidance, and safety-netting for recurrence
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Severe or crushing chest pain, worsening breathlessness, or syncope - call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of probable SVT and assessment of haemodynamic stability
Coaching a safe vagal manoeuvre (modified Valsalva) as first-line
Urgent in-person ECG capture and monitored termination (adenosine) if vagal fails
Advise assisted transport (not self-driving); 000 if unstable
High-dose caffeine cessation and cardiology/EP referral
acceptable_alternatives: Emergency department assessment is appropriate; a rapid-access arrhythmia service capturing the ECG in tachycardia is an acceptable alternative
'SVT', 'electrical short-circuit causing a fast rhythm' are acceptable framings
errors_of_omission: Not recognising SVT / attributing solely to anxiety-caffeine
No urgent ECG capture / termination pathway
No do-not-drive advice or instability safety-netting
errors_of_commission: Prescribing adenosine or other antiarrhythmics remotely
Advising the patient to drive himself while symptomatic
Telling the patient to simply wait it out indefinitely
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass requires (1) recognising stable SVT and assessing stability, (2) coaching a vagal manoeuvre and arranging urgent in-person ECG/termination if it fails, and (3) do-not-drive advice with instability safety-netting. Prescribing adenosine remotely, advising self-driving, or dismissing a sustained 185 bpm tachycardia as anxiety are failures.
